author: salvatore kettenbeil
text:
Absolutely the Best Chicken Around!

If you’re a chicken lover, this is the spot you’ve been searching for. From the very first bite, you can tell they take their food seriously — crispy on the outside, juicy and perfectly seasoned on the inside. Every piece is cooked to perfection, bursting with flavor that keeps you coming back for more.

The portions are generous, the service is fast and friendly, and the atmosphere just makes you want to stay a little longer. Whether it’s their fried chicken, grilled chicken, or their signature sauces — everything tastes fresh, homemade, and crafted with love.

I’ve tried chicken from all over, but nothing comes close to this. It’s honestly addicting. If you haven’t been here yet, do yourself a favor and go — your taste buds will thank you!

text:
I am writing to express my disappointment with my recent experience at this establishment. I visited for the first time and had high expectations, but unfortunately, my visit fell short. We arrived at 9:20 PM and were initially given the option to dine in, despite being told we could do so.

However, after requesting to dine in despite the online and physical signage clearly stating that the restaurant was open until 10:00 PM, we were informed that dine-in service was no longer available after 9:30 PM by the staff member who took our order. This information was not provided prior to us being seated, and as a result, we had already made the decision to dine in.

Furthermore, allowing other parties to dine in after us shortly after contradicted the information we were given. This treatment was not only confusing but also came across as inconsistent.

Additionally, when I returned to collect my food, I noticed that the sauce I had ordered was missing. Upon returning to the restaurant, I was assured by the staff member that the sauce was indeed available, but upon returning home, I found that it was indeed missing. This oversight not only resulted in my having to wait for my complete order but also potentially could have led to me paying extra for a sauce that was never provided

author: Branden
text:
We recently tried a chicken sandwich at Crimson Chicken and unfortunately had a disappointing experience. Initially, we were on a chicken tour and stopped by Raising Cane's first but decided to try Crimson Chicken due to their enticing social media advertising. While their chicken looked great, the taste left much to be desired. The sandwich was dry, overly seasoned, and lacked moisture despite the standard toppings. We opted for the Crimson Sandwich, priced at $12.23 after tax.

Shortly after eating, both my wife and I started feeling unwell. I had a persistent scratchy sensation in my throat for an hour, while my wife experienced stomach discomfort, as did I. Reflecting on it, we suspect the sandwich was excessively salty due to over-seasoning. The wait time was short, only 5-7 minutes, but it seemed like there was a lack of care in preparation. The bathroom was dirty, which was also concerning.

Initially, the customer service was okay, but when we called to report our issue, it took a turn for the worse. My wife was hung up on twice before finally speaking with a manager, who is also the owner. Unfortunately, the response was equally dismissive and rude. We were shocked by the lack of concern for food and customer safety.

We don't plan on returning due to the poor food quality and terrible customer service. We hope this review helps others make an informed decision and prevents someone from having a similar experience.

We ordered the Crimson Sandwich, and the slaw is creamy and well made. The pickles are ok, not needed as IMO not good together the slaw. Fries are well seasoned can’t go wrong with them. The staff were friendly. The only thing that I would say needs improvement would be the speed, perhaps we are spoiled with some of the comparable around. Reason being, it was not busy when we entered, and we waited good 15+ minutes waiting for the order, this is not going to be good in the long run. author: Stuff
text:
Absolutely phenomenal! Crimson Coward isn't just a Nashville hot chicken restaurant; it's an experience from start to finish, earning every single one of these five stars.
From the moment you walk in, the atmosphere sets the perfect tone. It's vibrant, energetic, and incredibly inviting, with a cool, modern twist on classic hot chicken joint vibes. You feel right at home, whether you're grabbing a quick bite or settling in for a satisfying meal.
But what truly elevates Crimson Coward is the staff. Every single person we encountered was genuinely friendly, welcoming, and incredibly efficient. They were happy to answer questions about the heat levels (which, trust me, are no joke!) and made excellent recommendations. Their passion for the food and commitment to excellent service truly shines through, making the entire dining experience even more enjoyable.
And then there's the food – oh, the food! Delicious doesn't even begin to cover it. The Nashville hot chicken is a masterpiece. Crispy on the outside, incredibly juicy and tender on the inside, and packed with flavor that goes beyond just heat. We tried a few different spice levels, and each one was perfectly balanced, allowing the incredible chicken to be the star. The sides are just as impressive, from the creamy mac and cheese to the tangy coleslaw, each complementing the chicken perfectly. You can tell they use high-quality, fresh ingredients, and it makes all the difference.
Crimson Coward is a must-visit for any hot chicken lover, or really, anyone looking for a truly delicious and memorable meal. This place is a gem, and we'll definitely be back for more!

author: Neil Naj
text:
Outstanding Experience at Crimson Coward! Fredericksburg, VA

From the moment we walked in, Crimson Coward delivered an incredible dining experience. The atmosphere is vibrant and welcoming — the perfect blend of casual and energetic, making it a great spot for both a quick bite and a laid-back hangout with friends.

Now, the food — absolutely phenomenal! The chicken is perfectly seasoned with just the right amount of heat and flavor, crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside. Every bite is packed with bold, delicious spices, and the sides were just as impressive. You can tell there’s real passion behind the menu.

The staff were friendly, attentive, and genuinely enthusiastic about making sure we had a great time. From helping us choose the right spice level to checking in with a smile, the customer service was top-tier.

Crimson Coward isn’t just a meal — it’s an experience. Highly recommend it to anyone looking for amazing food, great people, and an atmosphere that makes you want to come back again and again. Can’t wait for our next visit!

Neil.

text: When CC opened in Fredericksburg Va, I thought it was a decent place to grab a bite. I deliver DoorDash orders from time to time like many others do. I thought this was odd, but didn’t think too deeply about the interaction. On my second order I was accompanied by a white female dasher. Her order was called first, so I watched to see how her interaction would go. They put her order on the counter and walked away without holding the food until confirmed as done to me previously. Then I went to grab my order, and once again my order was held until I confirmed and showed my phone to them. So this time I spoke up to the all Hispanic crew asking why they felt the need to treat people differently depending on their race. At this time, they all stood speechless. It pissed me off and I can no longer support a culture that is bias to the customer. Do better better yall!

text: Crimson Coward offers five heat levels for their Nashville hot chicken, ranging from Country (no heat) to Mild, Medium, Hot (feel the burn), and the extreme BURRRN, baby burn for true heat seekers, with each level providing progressively more intense spice while retaining flavor, notes Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.

author: Unknown
text: Country: Full of flavor, zero heat, great for kids or spice-averse diners, say Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.  Mild: A subtle touch of heat, a hint of spice, according to Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.  Medium: A noticeable "slap of heat," perfect for those wanting flavor with a kick, note Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.  Hot: Where you start to "feel the burn," according to Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.  BURRRN, baby burn: The highest level, extremely spicy, designed for daredevils, state Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.
